Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to dark brown velvety spots on leaves and fruit; leaves may curl and drop prematurely; fruit develops scabby, cracked lesions.
Apple___Apple_scab.cure: Apply fungicide (captan or myclobutanil) at bud break and through petal fall. Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn. Prune for good air circulation. Plant scab-resistant varieties.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown to black circular lesions on fruit expanding outward; frogeye leaf spots (tan with dark borders); cankers on branches with reddish-brown bark.
Apple___Black_rot.cure: Prune out cankers and dead wood. Remove mummified fruit from trees and ground. Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl fungicide. Maintain tree vigor through proper fertilization.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright yellow-orange spots on upper leaf surfaces; tube-like structures on leaf undersides; fruit may develop similar spots and become deformed.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.cure: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb fungicide from pink bud through petal fall. Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees if possible (alternate host). Plant rust-resistant apple varieties.

Apple___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Apple___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular care including pruning, watering, and monitoring for early signs of disease.

Blueberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Blueberry___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Maintain acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5), consistent moisture, and annual pruning of old canes.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery fungal growth on leaves, shoots, and fruit; leaves curl upward and become distorted; fruit may crack or develop russeting.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.cure: Apply sulfur-based or myclobutanil fungicide at first sign. Prune for air circulation. Remove and destroy infected shoots. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue proper pruning, watering, and pest monitoring.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular grey to tan lesions running parallel to leaf veins; lesions may coalesce causing large areas of dead tissue; lower leaves affected first.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.cure: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate crops (avoid corn-on-corn). Till crop residue to reduce inoculum. Apply foliar fungicides (strobilurin or triazole) if disease pressure is high.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small, circular to elongated reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces; pustules may turn dark brown to black as the season progresses; heavy infection causes premature leaf death.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.cure: Plant resistant hybrids. Apply foliar fungicide (azoxystrobin or propiconazole) if disease appears before tasseling. Monitor fields regularly during cool, humid conditions.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long, elliptical grey-green to tan lesions (1-6 inches) on leaves; lesions may coalesce killing large leaf areas; reduced grain fill in severe cases.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.cure: Plant resistant hybrids (Ht genes). Rotate away from corn for 1-2 years. Apply foliar fungicide (propiconazole or pyraclostrobin) at early signs. Till crop residue.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Maintain proper spacing, fertilization, and irrigation schedules.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Tan circular leaf spots with dark borders; fruit develops brown rot then shrivels into hard black mummies; shoot lesions appear as dark elliptical cankers.
Grape___Black_rot.cure: Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil fungicide from bud break through veraison. Remove mummified fruit and infected canes. Ensure good canopy management for air circulation.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern on leaves (interveinal chlorosis and necrosis); dark spots on berries; sudden vine collapse in acute form; internal wood decay with dark streaking.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).cure: No proven cure. Remove severely affected vines. Protect pruning wounds with wound sealant. Avoid large pruning cuts. Remedial surgery (cutting out infected wood) may extend vine life.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Dark brown to black irregular spots on leaves; spots may have yellow halos; severe infection causes premature defoliation and reduced fruit quality.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.cure: Apply copper-based or mancozeb fungicide. Remove and destroy infected leaves. Improve canopy airflow through pruning. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Grape___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Grape___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular canopy management, balanced fertilization, and disease monitoring.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Asymmetric blotchy mottling of leaves; lopsided, bitter, small fruit that stays green at the stylar end; yellow shoots; premature fruit drop; tree decline over several years.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).cure: No cure exists. Remove and destroy infected trees to prevent spread. Control Asian citrus psyllid vector with systemic insecticides (imidacloprid). Plant certified disease-free nursery stock.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, angular, water-soaked spots on leaves that turn dark and may drop out (shot-hole appearance); fruit develops sunken, cracked lesions; twig cankers may form.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.cure: Apply oxytetracycline or copper-based bactericide during bloom and fruit development. Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain tree vigor with proper nutrition.

Peach___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Peach___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular pruning, thinning, and fungicide program for preventive care.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked lesions on leaves and fruit; lesions become raised and scabby; yellowing and premature leaf drop.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.cure: Apply copper-based bactericide. Remove and destroy infected plants. Avoid overhead watering. Use certified disease-free seeds and rotate crops every 2-3 years.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular watering, fertilisation, and monitoring.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings on lower leaves; premature defoliation; reduced tuber yield.
Potato___Early_blight.cure: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil. Remove infected leaves early. Avoid excess nitrogen fertiliser. Irrigate in the morning.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked dark lesions on leaves and tubers; white fungal growth on leaf undersides; rapid rotting of tubers in storage.
Potato___Late_blight.cure: Apply Metalaxyl or Cymoxanil fungicide. Destroy infected tubers. Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes. Hill soil around plants.

Potato___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Potato___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Maintain consistent watering and monitor for early signs of blight.

Raspberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Raspberry___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular pruning of spent canes, maintain good air circulation, and monitor for pests.

Soybean___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Soybean___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Maintain proper row spacing, crop rotation, and nutrient management.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery spots on upper and lower leaf surfaces; spots enlarge and coalesce covering entire leaves; leaves yellow, wither, and die prematurely.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.cure: Apply potassium bicarbonate, sulfur, or myclobutanil fungicide at first sign. Ensure good air circulation. Avoid overhead watering. Plant resistant varieties when available.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Irregular dark purple to brown spots on upper leaf surfaces; spots coalesce causing leaves to appear scorched; affected leaves dry up and curl at edges.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.cure: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Apply captan or copper-based fungicide. Renovate beds after harvest by mowing and thinning. Ensure adequate spacing for air circulation.

Strawberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Strawberry___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular watering, mulching, and runner management.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, greasy-looking spots on leaves, stems, and fruit; spots may have yellow halos; severe infection causes defoliation and fruit scabbing.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.cure: Apply copper-based bactericide early. Remove infected plant debris. Avoid overhead irrigation. Use disease-free transplants and rotate crops.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Brown circular spots with concentric rings (target pattern) on older leaves; yellowing around lesions; defoliation in severe cases.
Tomato___Early_blight.cure: Apply chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin fungicide. Remove infected leaves. Ensure good air circulation. Mulch around base to prevent soil splash.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems; white mold on leaf undersides; rapid browning and plant collapse in wet conditions.
Tomato___Late_blight.cure: Apply copper-based fungicide or Mancozeb 75WP. Remove and destroy infected plant material. Avoid overhead irrigation. Rotate crops annually.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale green to yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces; olive-green to brown velvety mold on leaf undersides; leaves curl and wither in severe cases.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.cure: Improve greenhouse ventilation and reduce humidity. Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide. Remove infected leaves. Avoid wetting foliage when watering.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Numerous small circular spots with dark borders and grey centres on lower leaves; tiny black fruiting bodies visible in spots; progressive defoliation from the bottom up.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.cure: Apply chlorothalonil or copper-based fungicide at first sign. Remove and destroy infected lower leaves. Mulch to prevent soil splash. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Fine stippling or yellow speckling on leaves; tiny webbing on leaf undersides; leaves become bronzed, dry, and brittle; severe infestation causes defoliation.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.cure: Spray with miticide (abamectin) or insecticidal soap. Increase humidity around plants. Introduce predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis). Remove heavily infested leaves.

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on leaves, stems, and fruit; spots enlarge and merge causing large necrotic areas; fruit lesions are sunken with concentric rings.
Tomato___Target_Spot.cure: Apply azoxystrobin or chlorothalonil fungicide. Prune lower branches for air circulation. Remove plant debris after harvest. Rotate crops for 2-3 years.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves; stunted plant growth; reduced fruit set; leaves become thick, leathery, and crumpled.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.cure: No cure for infected plants -- remove and destroy them. Control whitefly vectors with insecticides (imidacloprid) or sticky traps. Use resistant tomato varieties. Install fine mesh netting.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mottled light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves; leaf distortion and curling; stunted growth; uneven fruit ripening with brown streaks.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.cure: No cure -- remove and destroy infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution. Wash hands before handling plants. Use virus-resistant seed varieties. Avoid tobacco use near plants.

Tomato___healthy.symptoms: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy.
Tomato___healthy.cure: No treatment required. Continue regular watering, fertilisation, and monitoring.
